scope: Migrate every spec-v1-era release record (spec.json + status.json + board.json) to the strict baton v0.10.0 contract via a committed re-runnable idempotent script, preserve EARS classification by mapping the retired AC type to ears_pattern and repointing the ears.go reader (sworn#95), re-render the five index.md, then remove the S11 read-path normalise shim wholesale so the reader enforces v0.10.0 strictly (sworn#90).

test_results.output_ref: 47 packages ok, 0 FAIL — full suite green after shim removal (the AC-04 fixture gate)
AC-03/AC-06/AC-07: 33 spec.json + 5 board.json validate against v0.10.0 spec-v1/board-v1 (draft-2020-12); EARS classification preserved on migrated data
AC-04: EffortComplexity.Validate strictly quick/grind/puzzle/beast; TestReadRejectsRetiredQuadrant proves Read fails closed on chore/epic
idempotent re-run: 0 'migrated' lines, all postconditions satisfied (chore/epic/feature=0, spec.json in_scope/out_of_scope present, no schema_version)
DESIGNFIT PASS — 15 slice(s); the Type-1 ears_pattern decision carries the Coach human_decision
board renders; S12 implemented; oracle reads the migrated pure-plan board.json

reachability.evidence: AC-07 sworn#95 reachability (freshly-built binary): `sworn lint ac 2026-06-28-driver-contract` over the MIGRATED records classifies ubiquitous=41, event-driven=29, state-driven=1, unwanted-behaviour=11, total=82 — NOT the all-Ubiquitous(82) degradation the stale ears_keyword reader produced. Proves the type->ears_pattern migration + the ears.go reader repoint preserve EARS classification end-to-end. Backed by TestRecordsConformance_EARSClassificationPreserved (asserts event-driven>0 and unwanted-behaviour>0 on the real migrated release). Render reachability (AC-05, re-verified live 2026-07-11 after re-rendering driver-contract's index.md at the slice's committed `implemented` state): `sworn render` on all five is idempotent (driver-contract index.md md5 96cf8795 stable across a second render). Live `sworn doctor` render-drift now reports `2 of 7 board.json-backed release(s) drifted or failed to render` — flagging ONLY the two excluded legacy releases (2026-06-19-safe-parallelism: missing S01-process-ownership spec.json; 2026-06-27-conformance-foundation: bare-string board.json). None of the five spec-v1-era releases (driver-contract included) drift. The first pass had rendered driver-contract's committed index.md at `in_progress` while the slice's committed state advanced to `implemented`; this re-render clears that single-cell drift (quadrant `grind` was already correct).

delivered.evidence: grep -rn '"quadrant": "feature"' docs/release/ --include='*.json' = 0; script postcondition asserts chore/epic/feature=0

delivered.evidence: 5 index.md re-rendered (0 chore/epic tokens, was 6/6/3/2/7); render idempotent (index.md md5 96cf8795 stable). Live `sworn doctor` render-drift = `2 of 7`, flagging ONLY the 2 excluded legacy releases (2026-06-19-safe-parallelism, 2026-06-27-conformance-foundation); none of the five drift. driver-contract's single-cell drift (committed index.md `in_progress` vs render `implemented`) cleared by this re-render

divergence.description: One test file beyond declared touchpoints: internal/baton/records_conformance_test.go — the AC-03/AC-06 strict-schema sweep mechanism (no CLI runs baton.ValidateSchema over on-disk records). Planned in design.md P-3 and Captain-acknowledged (review pin 3); recorded as a Type-2 design_decision.

AC-01/AC-02 assertions captured JSON-scoped (grep --include='*.json'): the only hits from the bare `grep -rn ... docs/release/` are prose in THIS slice's own design.md and review.md that quote the JSON strings while documenting the migration (0 record hits). This follows the Captain's own review.md precedent; the migrated records — the thing the ACs are about — carry zero chore/epic/feature quadrants.

AC-02 landed as a zero-match feature-quadrant GUARD rather than a 'correct the single feature record' edit: zero feature-quadrant records exist in JSON form (verified). Coach-ratified amendment (captain-proceed.md pin 2), already reflected in spec.json.
